author: doll-agent
content:
Done. Existing single-element tests covered the var ddof=0/ddof=1 split (var_single_element_ddof0 returns 0.0, var_single_element_ddof1_errors confirms ferray rejects ddof>=N strictly while NumPy returns NaN). Added 4 more for completeness:

- std_single_element_ddof1_errors: confirms std_ delegates the error through (parity with var)
- var_two_elements_ddof1_population_to_sample: verifies Bessel's correction (var with ddof=1 = 2x var with ddof=0 for N=2 — the population/sample relationship)
- var_single_element_2d_ddof0: 1×1 array — exercises the multi-D codepath with a single element
- var_single_element_axis_ddof_too_large_errors: 1×3 array reduced along axis 0 (length 1) with ddof=1 — confirms the ddof>=axis_len guard fires on the axis path too

247 ferray-stats lib tests pass, all single-element edge cases covered.
